basics.summary: Currently employed at Red Brick University. Short biography for the left-hand sidebar

work.summary: Vancouver, BC. Alexa Daily Essentials team.
Vancouver, BC. Agentic workflows for Alexa+
Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. CPSC 532C/554C: Topics in Artificial Intelligence: Human-Centred AI by Professor Cristina Conati.
Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. CPSC 430: Computers and Society by Professor Kevin Leyton-Brown.

Conducted a literature review of existing image classification machine learning models. Curated a comprehensive dataset for training and evaluating the chosen model. Created a robust and light-weight Image Content Filtration tool for user-submitted edits (to counter vandalism attacks) using computer vision (transfer learning). Developed a RESTful API for alpha testing. Experimented with model compression techniques such as layer-based pruning and knowledge distillation. Wrote the relevant technical and non-technical documentation.

Auckland, New Zealand. Supervisor: Professor Helen Sword. Conducted an extensive study on researcher development frameworks and initiatives across research-intensive universities around the world. Drew parallels on features and development solutions for research between ECRs and PGRs. Developed a thorough scoring system that takes into account broad categories of researcher development activities. Performed data analysis to draw inferences and lay out conclusions.

Singapore. Area of work: Big Data Analytics using Artificial Neural Networks. Worked on the Driver Distraction Detection problem using video feeds to develop a binary classifier. Tested custom CNNs with transfer learning for classification. Used image filtering and feature extraction techniques using R and Python.
